question: What is the user's desired direction and completion criteria for proving DinoBrain memory-on beats memory-off, including golden sets, Ragas-like evaluation, behavior gates, trust score, regression tests, and observability?

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented and deployed DinoBrain v2.0.1 stability fixes based on the QA review. Patched fail-closed Codex hook duplicate-lock behavior, added Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook registration and verification, expanded v2 action gates to the full OS tool contract, made behavior eval strict when golden cases are missing, added source chunk redaction/truncation guards, made node lifecycle apply real archive/hold/merge/provenance repair operations, handled os_begin_task context-pack failures as fail-closed task/gate records, fixed Observatory JSON graph fallback for v2 indexes, bumped app/installer version to 2.0.1, added regression coverage, pushed main and tag v2.0.1. Local release ZIP was built; direct GitHub Release upload could not run because no GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN is available in this shell.

Bumped DinoBrain to v2.2.1 and pushed main/tag.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Explained that Codex hook trust approval should not be automated because it is the security boundary for command-running hooks. Verified local DinoBrain docs/code state: installer can register user-level hooks, enable hook runtime, run an installed-hook handshake, and provide diagnostics, but cannot bypass Codex's hook trust prompt.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Fixed installer Codex UserPromptSubmit registration so DinoBrain is merged into the first existing prompt-hook group instead of being appended as a separate second group. Only install.ps1 and scripts/verify-installer-hooks.ps1 were changed. Added regression cases preserving why-was-fable-banned and verifying DinoBrain appears exactly once in the first UserPromptSubmit group. Created branch fix/codex-userprompt-hook-merge, committed, pushed, and opened PR #1.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep user trust approval and data sync manual-review boundaries intact.
items.summary: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep user trust approval and data sync manual-review boundaries intact. | Evidence: Keep user trust approval and data sync manual-review boundaries intact.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented a post-install Codex hook approval flow. The installer now creates DinoBrain Codex Hook Approval.cmd and launches it after hook registration unless skipped. The helper detects stale Codex desktop processes, restarts/reopens Codex, copies /hooks to the clipboard, and shows instructions while preserving the requirement that the user manually trusts the hook. Updated the Windows setup UI with a checked-by-default approval flow option, added verification, updated uninstall cleanup, bumped version to 0.1.4, pushed main/tag, uploaded the v0.1.4 release ZIP, and verified the uploaded private asset hash.

items.title: Behavior rule: Do not forge or bypass Codex hook trust approval; treat it as a required user security decision.
items.summary: Behavior rule: Do not forge or bypass Codex hook trust approval; treat it as a required user security decision. | Evidence: Do not forge or bypass Codex hook trust approval; treat it as a required user security decision.
